title: cyclamen purpurascens subsp. purpurascens
intro: Cyclamen purpurascens subsp. purpurascens, also known as the wild cyclamen, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the primrose family and is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant's tubers and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and wounds. In terms of health benefits, Cyclamen purpurascens subsp. purpurascens has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
